name: Estrella Immunopharma, Inc.
business: Estrella Immunopharma, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing T-cell therapies for cancers and autoimmune diseases. Its lead product candidate, EB103, is a CD19-directed ARTEMIS T-cell therapy currently in a Phase I/II clinical trial for relapsed or refractory B-cell non-Hodgkin's lymphoma. The company also develops EB104 (dual-targeting CD19/CD22) and explores a mark-and-kill strategy with Imugene's CF33-CD19t oncolytic virus for solid tumors.
sellsTo: patients with blood cancers and solid tumors

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing T-cell therapies to address treatment challenges for patients with cancers and autoimmune diseases.

Our innovative ARTEMIS T Cell Receptor Platform uniquely designs T cells that, unlike a traditional CAR-T cell, are activated and regulated upon engagement with cancer targets using cellular mechanisms more resembling those from the endogenous T-cell receptor (TCR).

catalysts.event: Completion of Phase I dose-escalation portion of STARLIGHT-1 trial
catalysts.sentence: As of December 2025, we have completed the second dose cohort in the Phase I dose-escalation portion of our STARLIGHT-1 Phase I/II clinical trial of EB103, and an independent Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) has completed its review of safety data from the Phase I dose-escalation phase (n=9) and recommended advancing the trial into the Phase II expansion phase at the recommended Phase II dose (RP2D).

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase II expansion phase of STARLIGHT-1 trial
catalysts.sentence: Patient dosing for Phase II has commenced in January 2026 to further evaluate EB103 at RP2D in a larger patient cohort to confirm the Phase I results.

catalysts.event: Anticipated completion of Phase II portion of STARLIGHT-1 trial
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate completing the Phase II portion of the STARLIGHT-1 clinical trial in 1H2027.

tamClaims.claim: Global CAR T-cell therapy market size estimated at $5.82 billion in 2025 and projected to reach $22.36 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 18.06% from 2026 to 2033.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Grand View Research, the global CAR T-cell therapy market size was estimated at $5.82 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $22.36 billion by 2033, growing at a compounded annual growth rate of 18.06% from 2026 to 2033.

tamClaims.claim: Currently only about 20% of eligible patients are treated by current CAR-T therapies.
tamClaims.sentence: However, currently only about 20% of eligible patients are treated by current CAR-T therapies, due to their various limitations described above.

tamClaims.claim: Solid tumors represent approximately 1,600,000 new cancer cases, or 90% of total cancer diagnoses in the United States, each year.
tamClaims.sentence: Solid tumors represent approximately 1,600,000 new cancer cases, or 90% of total cancer diagnoses in the United States, each year.
